summary: Flatten built-in kind identity onto the lock row label — project its 3 kind rows, drop agents-md.memory and the provider-qualified labels; closes (builtin-workspace-qualified-key)

per.section: Decision: built-ins are a module, and identity is an import

edit.description: definitions()/definition()/qualified()/features() source the kind set from the parsed built-in lock's `KindFactRow`s (memory, rule, skill — no provider column), not the hand-written CustomKind literals; drop `BUILTIN_KINDS` (the `<provider>.<name>` roster), `agents_md_memory()`, and the resolve_bare/AmbiguousKind bare→qualified path. Bare name == lock row label == unique identity; `qualified(name)` returns the bare name. Drop the dangling doc-comment refs to retired spec titles ("A built-in kind is an adapter", "kind identity carries a provider axis", "Placement mirrors identity") and the `kinds/<provider>/<name>/KIND.md` tree refs (rust.md exit clause).

`CustomKind::qualified_name()` returns the bare label; retire (or reduce to inert) `CustomKind.qualified`, `CustomKind::resolve_bare`, and `KindError::AmbiguousKind` — the migration-era bare-name resolution machinery (20-surface.md, "The migration-era bare-name resolution machinery retires").

Rebind the floors by bare kind label — drop `QUALIFIED_FLOOR_BINDINGS`, `floor_package(qualified)`'s provider indirection, and the `agents-md.memory`→`memory.agents-md` binding (agents-md dropped). Hand-written clause DATA stays until BUILTIN-FLOOR-LOCK-PROJECTION; only the binding flattens.
The check floor dispatch (builtin_floors/BUILTIN_FLOOR_BINDINGS :66-84, the :573-602 per-kind loop) keys floors by kind row label; drop builtin_kind::qualified.
Floor resolution (:149-170) keys by kind row label; drop the builtin::floor_package + builtin_kind::qualified chaining.
effective() drops the qualified→bare rsplit (:107-110) now that floors bind by bare label.
install.rs:275 keys its members map by kind.qualified_name() — now the bare label; and any other qualified-string consumer (import.rs comment refs) updates to the flat label.

Workspace keys members by the row label (the kind name is now the unique label); confirm skills()/rules() stay correct — two same-label rows are a malformed lock, not a collision to resolve. Closes (builtin-workspace-qualified-key).

tests.asserts: drop the two-provider/qualified-identity assertions (retired machinery); keep the frontmatterless File-shape memory round-trip over the single `memory` kind
drop the un-double-reported-across-two-providers framing; one memory kind, the max_lines advisory fires/stays-silent once

acceptance: builtin_kind projects the built-in lock's 3 kind rows; no agents-md.memory, no `<provider>.<name>` identity; kind labels are the bare, unique row labels; check::Workspace keys by the row label. (builtin-workspace-qualified-key) closes. Ripple to the read_verbs snapshots + rule_contract.rs (floor names shift off `.anthropic`) lands with BUILTIN-FLOOR-LOCK-PROJECTION.
notes: The migration's core, large but one idea (remove the provider axis). Drops engine AGENTS.md coverage (glob **/AGENTS.md) to match the module/lock — 15-kinds memory locus is **/CLAUDE.md; whether temper ships a distinctly-labelled AGENTS.md kind later is (agents-md-builtin-kind), a feature add, not a blocker. Serialize BUILTIN-FLOOR-LOCK-PROJECTION behind it (shared builtin.rs/main.rs/read.rs/compose.rs).

summary: Reimplement builtin.rs's floor data as a projection of the built-in lock's clause rows (guidance+cite carried) — no hand-written clause vectors; retire the package noun

per.section: Decision: the built-in lock is derived from the SDK module, never transcribed

edit.description: contract()/contracts() build each kind's floor Contract from the parsed built-in lock's ClauseRows (predicate·field·severity·guidance·cite) instead of the hand-written skill_anthropic()/rule_anthropic()/memory_anthropic() clause vectors and the *_GUIDANCE consts; drop SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE/MEMORY_ANTHROPIC_PACKAGE and BUILTIN_PACKAGE_NAMES. Floors keyed by kind row label. Retire the `package std-lib`/PACKAGE.md doc comments (rust.md exit clause).
Any residual package-name floor lookup in the check dispatch becomes a kind-label lookup over the projection (most already flattened by BUILTIN-KIND-FLATTEN).
The `explain` floor-name rendering (:149-170, :409-413) names the floor by its kind label, not a `<kind>.<source>` package name.

tests.asserts: the rule floor is the lock-projected contract keyed by the `rule` kind; its display name is the kind label, not `rule.anthropic`; declared severities and guidance survive the projection
the governing-floor line names the floor by its kind label (the `.anthropic` package names are gone); why_collaboration/why_lint_runner snapshots update alike

acceptance: builtin.rs carries no hand-written clause or guidance data — every built-in floor is a projection of the embedded lock's clause rows (predicate/severity/field/guidance/cite); the package noun and its `<kind>.<source>` names are gone; contract_template.rs/acceptance.rs floor-name assertions update; gates green.
notes: Second consumer link. Needs LOCK-CLAUSE-CHANNELS (guidance+cite in the rows) and BUILTIN-KIND-FLATTEN (flat kind labels, package binding removed). Contract-the-Rust-type stays (code owns the type name; a rename is out of scope). Closes the derived-lock Decision's `no hand-written mirror of the SDK's floors`.

summary: check models only the built-in kinds — read the committed lock's custom kind rows so custom-kind requirements resolve instead of false admissibility failures (cascade field report)

per.section: The lock and drift — one vocabulary

edit.description: The gate builds its kind set from the built-in default program only and reads the committed lock (`drift::read_declarations`) for clauses/requirements/satisfies but NOT for kinds — so an adopted harness's `declarations.kinds` (its custom kind rows, written whole by emit — cascade saw 9 spec kinds + 74 members in a fresh lock) are invisible, and a requirement naming a custom kind fails `requirement.admissibility` ("names kind X, which temper does not model") with an `unfilled` shadow. Feed the committed lock's `KindFactRow`s into the check kind loop, merged over the built-in default program, so a custom-kind requirement resolves against its declared kind.

`Workspace::load_kinds` / the members-map keying must load members for the committed lock's custom kind rows (their loci/layout), not only the built-in kind set, so a custom kind's members are conformance-checked (cascade: `check --harness .` checked only the built-ins).

Kind-blind extraction over the committed lock's custom kind facts (locus, layout, registration) — the same generic path the built-in kinds ride, sourced from the lock rows. Reassess whether any engine.rs edit is needed once the chain lands: extraction may already be fully generic and the fix confined to check.rs/main.rs feeding it the rows.

tests.asserts: a harness whose committed lock declares a custom kind and a requirement naming it does NOT emit a false requirement.admissibility failure; the requirement resolves against the lock-declared kind and reports genuine coverage only

acceptance: `temper check` (and its session-start reporter) reads custom kind declaration rows from the committed lock, so a custom-kind requirement resolves against its declared kind instead of failing admissibility as unmodeled; the session-start gate no longer cries wolf on any harness with a custom-kind requirement.
notes: Cascade field report, 07-06 — the false blockers made cascade disable its SessionStart hook (law-3 'gate that cries wolf gets disabled'). Serialized behind the derived-lock chain: it edits the same check/main kind-resolution code the chain reworks. When the chain ships, plan re-checks whether the row-driven path already reads the committed lock's custom kinds — if so, drop this.

summary: Whole-tree comment sweep — prose spec recaps to pointer tags, delete era + compliance narration per rust.md's taxonomy; no behavior change

gate.reason: Whole-tree sweep — must run solo (a broad src/+tests/ comment edit races any concurrent entry). Ride after the derived-lock chain lands: LOCK-CLAUSE-CHANNELS / BUILTIN-KIND-FLATTEN / BUILTIN-FLOOR-LOCK-PROJECTION rewrite the two heaviest-commented files (builtin.rs/builtin_kind.rs) on contact, so sweeping them first is throwaway. Plan promotes this to a solo open entry once the chain ships and the queue is otherwise empty.

edit.description: Representative anchor — the true scope is all of src/ and tests/. Per rust.md's taxonomy + exit clause: convert prose spec recaps to terse pointer tags (file path only, no section titles — a quoted section title is a dangling reference), delete era narration ('no longer X', git owns history), compliance narration ('per §X we…'), and dangling pending-tag references. Module //! headers to ~10 lines (overview + pointer).
Representative anchor for the same sweep across the crate's other over-commented modules and their tests.

acceptance: Zero quoted spec section titles in src/ or tests/ comments; zero era vocabulary from 90-spec-system's migration trail; no comment referencing a pending tag that isn't in the queue; module //! headers ~10 lines; cargo gates green — no behavior change.
notes: Ceremony item (John 07-06). rust.md's exit clause cuts comments on contact for files the chain touches; this sweep is the residue in the files the chain leaves untouched, run once at the end. Deferred (not blockedBy) because a whole-tree edit races every concurrent entry — plan promotes it to a solo open entry after the derived-lock chain fully lands and the queue is otherwise empty.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
